CannaScope CT Beta Version 4 — macOS
====================================
If macOS says "Run CannaScope CT.command" cannot be opened or is from an
unidentified developer, that is NORMAL (it's just blocking a downloaded
script). Do ONE of these:

OPTION 1 (easiest): Right-click "Run CannaScope CT.command" -> Open -> Open.
   (Newer macOS: try once, then System Settings > Privacy & Security >
    "Open Anyway".)
OPTION 2: Open Terminal, type:  xattr -cr
   then drag this folder onto Terminal and press Return. Then double-click
   "Run CannaScope CT.command".

First run sets up Python deps (needs Python 3.9+) and then scans the last 60
days of products. When it finishes, your numbered report appears as:
   CannaScope CT Beta Version 4 - Flagged Products - N.pdf   (a NEW number each run)
right next to this file, with full data in:
   CannaScope CT - Flagged Product Results and Sources/

Every flag is a LEAD to verify against the product's COA (its number is a
clickable link in the report). Report misreads:
github.com/jmlschlee/Connecticut-Cannabis-Contaminant-Checker-Beta-V1/issues
